questions.text: When a body of weight ‘W’ is kept suspended by a string, the weight of the body pulls the string downwards, while the string pulls the body upwards with an equal force, this force is called?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The frequency of a string refers to how often it vibrates per unit time and is related to sound waves, not the static forces on a suspended body.
Displacement refers to the change in position of an object. For a string, this is relevant when considering vibrations, not when analyzing static forces.
Tension is the force carried by a string when it is pulled by forces acting from opposite ends. It balances the weight of the suspended body by acting upwards, making it the correct answer.
The wavelength is a measure of the distance between successive crests of a wave and pertains to wave properties, not to the forces involved in suspending a body.

questions.explanation: When a body is suspended by a string, the downward gravitational force (weight) is balanced by an upward force called tension. This tension is equal in magnitude to the weight of the body, keeping the system in equilibrium. The incorrect options (frequency, displacement, wavelength) all pertain to aspects of wave motion and vibrations, which are unrelated to the static equilibrium scenario described in the question.

questions.text: When a body is moving in an inertial frame of reference, it has
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a body is moving in an inertial frame of reference, it means that there are no external forces acting on the body. In such a scenario, according to Newton's first law of motion, the body will continue to move with a constant velocity (including zero velocity if it was initially at rest) unless acted upon by an external force. Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity. If there are no external forces acting on the body, the velocity of the body will remain constant, and therefore, the rate of change of velocity (acceleration) will be zero. Therefore, the correct answer is A: zero acceleration, indicating that the body maintains a constant velocity in the absence of external forces.

This option is incorrect because constant acceleration would indicate the presence of a net external force acting on the body. In an inertial frame, the absence of such forces means the acceleration must be zero.
This option is incorrect because increasing acceleration suggests an increasing net force. In an inertial frame of reference, there are no external forces, so the acceleration is zero.

This option is incorrect as well. Decreasing acceleration would imply the presence of an external force that is diminishing but not absent. In an inertial frame, no external forces act, so acceleration remains zero.

questions.explanation: When a body is moving in an inertial frame of reference, it means that there are no external forces acting on the body. In such a scenario, according to Newton's first law of motion, the body will continue to move with a constant velocity (including zero velocity if it was initially at rest) unless acted upon by an external force. Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity. If there are no external forces acting on the body, the velocity of the body will remain constant, and therefore, the rate of change of velocity (acceleration) will be zero. Therefore, the correct answer is A: zero acceleration, indicating that the body maintains a constant velocity in the absence of external forces. and with respect to an inertial frame, an object or body accelerates only when a physical force is applied, and (following Newton's first law of motion), in the absence of a net force, a body at rest will remain at rest and a body in motion will continue to move uniformly—that is, in a straight line and at constant ...

This is incorrect as per explanation.Initial velocity, J = 15m / sFinal velocity, v = 0m / sDistance, s = 18m, Acceleration, a=?Using relation, v2 - u2 = 2as 02 - (15)2= 2a×18- 225 = 36aa = -225/ 36 = - 6.25 m /s2So, deceleration is 6.25m/s² which is equal to 6.3m/s².

questions.text: A cyclist moves along a circular path of radius 70m. If he completes one round in 11s, the total length of a path is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per the formula
The following is the solution:The radius of the circular path, r=70mTime takes to complete one round, t=11sThe total length of the path, s=2πr =2×(22/7)×70=440m.
Incorrect as per the formula
Incorrect as per the formula

questions.explanation: The following is the solution:The radius of the circular path, r=70mTime takes to complete one round, t=11sThe total length of the path, s=2πr =2×(22/7)×70=440m.

questions.text: Third equation of motion is independent of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Time does not appear in the third equation of motion, which involves final velocity, initial velocity, acceleration, and displacement. Therefore, the equation is independent of time.
Displacement is a crucial variable in the third equation of motion. It is necessary to determine the relationship between the velocities and acceleration, so it cannot be independent of the equation.
Velocity, including both initial and final velocities, is integral to the third equation of motion. It directly affects the calculation, making it dependent on the equation.
Acceleration is a fundamental part of the third equation of motion, impacting the change in velocity over a given displacement. Thus, it is not independent of the equation.

questions.explanation: The third equation of motion is expressed as vf2 - vi2 = 2as, where vf is the final velocity, vi is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and s is the displacement. Notably, this equation does not include the variable of time, which makes it independent of time. Other variables such as displacement, velocity, and acceleration are explicitly part of this equation, making them dependent on it.

questions.text: The displacement of the moving body shown in the following figure is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per the formula

The following is the solution: The image shows three rectangles, with the following dimensions: Rectangle 1: 8m x 4m Rectangle 2: 2m x 3m Rectangle 3: 6m x 3m To calculate the displacement, we need to find the distance between these two points. d² = x² + y² In this case, the difference in the x-coordinates of the two points is 8m - 2m = 6m, and the difference in the y-coordinates of the two points is 4m - 3m = 1m. d² = 6² + 1² = 37 Taking the square root of both sides, we get: d = √37 = 6.08m ≈ 7.
Incorrect as per the formula
Incorrect as per the formula

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: The image shows three rectangles, with the following dimensions: Rectangle 1: 8m x 4m Rectangle 2: 2m x 3m Rectangle 3: 6m x 3m To calculate the displacement, we need to find the distance between these two points. d² = x² + y² In this case, the difference in the x-coordinates of the two points is 8m - 2m = 6m, and the difference in the y-coordinates of the two points is 4m - 3m = 1m. d² = 6² + 1² = 37Taking the square root of both sides, we get: d = √37 = 6.08m≈ 7.

questions.text: When we jump out a boat standing in water, it moves
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer because, according to Newton's third law of motion, the force exerted by the person jumping forward results in an equal and opposite reaction force on the boat, causing it to move backward.
This option is incorrect. The boat cannot move forward because the person jumping forward applies a backward force on the boat, resulting in it moving in the opposite direction.
This option is incorrect. Sideways movement would require a lateral force, which is not present in the action of jumping directly out of the boat.
This option is incorrect. The scenario clearly describes an action and reaction pair according to Newton's third law, which dictates that the boat will move backward.

questions.explanation: According to Newton's third law of motion, every action has an equal and opposite reaction. When a person jumps out of a boat in water, they exert a force on the boat in the opposite direction of their jump. This causes the boat to move backward. The backward movement is the reaction to the force exerted by the jumper. The other options are incorrect because they do not adhere to the principles of action and reaction forces as described by Newton's third law.

questions.text: A ball of mass 1 kg is accelerating at the rate of 1 m s-2. The rate of change of momentum is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: F = Δp/ Δt F = mΔv / Δt a = Δv / Δt F = 1 x 1 F = 1 kg m s-2 Rate of change of momentum is called force
This option is incorrect. The force is calculated as F = ma, which for the given values is 1 kg m s-2, not 2 kg m s-2.
This option is incorrect. The force calculation using F = ma gives 1 kg m s-2, not 3 kg m s-2.
This option is incorrect. The correct force is 1 kg m s-2, not 4 kg m s-2, as determined by F = ma.

questions.explanation: According to Newton's second law of motion, the rate of change of momentum is equal to the force applied to an object. The force can be calculated using the formula F = ma, where m is the mass in kilograms and a is the acceleration in meters per second squared. For this question, m = 1 kg and a = 1 m s-2, so the force (rate of change of momentum) is F = 1 kg x 1 m s-2 = 1 kg m s-2.Option A is correct because it provides the accurate calculation of the rate of change of momentum. Options B, C, and D are incorrect because they provide incorrect values for the force based on the given data.

questions.text: Two similar spheres, each of mass m and travelling with speed v, are moving towards each other.The spheres have a head on elastic collision. Which statement is correct?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The spheres stick together on impact.
options.explanation: When colliding objects stick together on impact, it is an inelastic collision.

options.text: The total kinetic energy after impact is mv2 .
options.explanation: Total K.E before impact = 1/2mv2 + 1/2mv2 = mv2 Since, it is an elastic collision total K.E is conserved, and it remains mv2 after the impact aswell.

options.text: The total kinetic energy before impact is zero
options.explanation: Total K.E before impact = 1/2mv2 + 1/2mv2= mv2 ; hence, it is not zero.

options.text: The total momentum before impact is 2 mv
options.explanation: Total momentum before impact= mv + (-mv) = m(v-v) = m(0) = 0 As shown by the calculation above, the total momentum before impact is not 2mv; it is 0 as both of the spheres have the same magnitude of velocity and mass but are moving in opposite directions.

questions.explanation: Total K.E before impact = 1/2mv2 + 1/2mv2 = mv2 Since, it is an elastic collision total K.E is conserved, and it remains mv2 after the impact aswell.

questions.text: SI unit of impulse is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the SI unit of force, not impulse. It represents a kilogram meter per second squared, which is a Newton (N).
The following is the solution: J=change in momentum J=mv2-mv1 J=kg x ms-1 F=ma N=kg x ms-2 J=Ns Option B is correct because the SI unit of impulse is the Newton-second (Ns).
This unit represents the reciprocal of a Newton-second, which is not applicable to impulse.
This is the unit for torque or work, representing Newton-meter, which is not related to impulse.

questions.explanation: The following is the solution: J=change in momentum J=mv2-mv1 J=kg x ms-1 F=ma N=kg x ms-2 J=Ns Option B is correct because the SI unit of impulse is the Newton-second (Ns).

questions.text: The area between the velocity-time graph is numerically equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. The area under a velocity-time graph does not give velocity itself, but rather a quantity derived from it.
Correct. The area between the velocity-time graph and the time axis represents the displacement of the object over the given time interval.
Incorrect. Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity, not represented by the area under a velocity-time graph.
Incorrect. Time is the independent variable on the velocity-time graph and is not represented by the area under the graph.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Displacement. In a velocity-time graph, the area under the curve between the graph and the time axis gives the displacement of the object. This is because displacement is calculated as the integral of velocity with respect to time. Option A, Velocity, is incorrect because the graph already represents velocity, and the area under it provides displacement, not velocity itself. Option C, Acceleration, is incorrect because acceleration is related to the slope of the velocity-time graph, not the area under it. Option D, Time, is incorrect because time is represented on the horizontal axis and is not a result of the area calculation.

questions.text: If you throw a ball downward, then its acceleration immediately after leaving your hand, assuming no air resistance, is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer because in the absence of air resistance, the only force acting on the ball is gravity, which gives it a constant acceleration of 9.8 m/s² downward.
This is incorrect because gravity provides a constant acceleration of 9.8 m/s² downward, regardless of the initial velocity of the ball.
This is incorrect because the only force acting on the ball immediately after it is released is gravity, which causes a constant acceleration of 9.8 m/s².

options.text: Speed of throw is required for answer
options.explanation: This is incorrect because the speed of the throw affects the initial velocity, not the acceleration, which is solely determined by gravity in the absence of air resistance.

questions.explanation: In the absence of air resistance, the only force acting on a freely falling object is gravity. This results in a constant acceleration of 9.8 m/s² downward, which is independent of the initial velocity or the speed at which the object is thrown. Therefore, option A is correct. Options B and C are incorrect because they suggest a deviation from the constant gravitational acceleration. Option D is incorrect because the acceleration due to gravity does not depend on the speed of the throw.

questions.text: The quantitative measure of inertia of a body is its:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Mass is the quantitative measure of an object's inertia. It is a constant property that determines how much an object resists changes to its motion regardless of its environment.
Weight is the force exerted by gravity on an object. It varies with the gravitational field and is not a measure of inertia, as it depends on location.
Velocity is a vector quantity describing an object's speed and direction of motion. It does not relate to the object's resistance to changes in motion.
Momentum is the product of an object's mass and velocity. While mass is part of momentum, momentum itself is not a direct measure of inertia but rather a measure of motion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Mass because it represents the quantitative measure of inertia, indicating an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion. Mass remains constant regardless of an object's location. Weight, in contrast, depends on gravitational pull and varies with location. Velocity describes how fast and in what direction an object moves, but it does not measure resistance to motion. Momentum combines mass and velocity to describe motion but is not solely a measure of inertia.

questions.text: A rocket works on basis of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton's first law addresses the concept of inertia, where an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force. While important in understanding motion, it does not explain the propulsion mechanism of rockets, which involves interactions between forces.

Newton's second law establishes the relationship between force, mass, and acceleration, indicating how an object's motion changes under a net force. Although relevant to calculating the acceleration of a rocket, it does not directly describe the principle of propulsion through action and reaction.

Newton's third law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is crucial for rocket propulsion: the expulsion of exhaust gases backward (action) results in a forward thrust (reaction), propelling the rocket.

Newton's gravitational law explains the attractive force between masses, such as the Earth and a rocket. While gravity is significant in determining a rocket's trajectory, it is not the mechanism by which a rocket propels itself forward.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Newton's third law of motion, which states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This principle is fundamental to rocket propulsion: the action of expelling exhaust gases downward produces an opposite reaction that propels the rocket upward.Newton's first law concerns inertia and does not involve the action-reaction pairs that drive rocket motion. Newton's second law describes how force acts on mass to produce acceleration, but it does not specifically explain the mechanism of action and reaction in propulsion. Newton's gravitational law explains how gravity affects the motion of objects, influencing trajectory but not providing the mechanism for rocket propulsion.

questions.text: A bus and a car, moving at the same speed, are brought to rest by applying the same retarding force then:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Bus will come to rest in a shorter distance
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as bus having more mass and hence more inertia will come to rest after the car.

options.text: Car will come to rest in a shorter distance
options.explanation: The distance they move will depend on their deceleration, which in turn would depend on the frictional force exerted by their respective brakes. If both were to experience the same frictional force 'F', then as per F=ma it is evident that the car having the lesser mass will decelerate faster ( greater magnitude of deceleration) and will therefore come to a halt sooner. Conceptually this is due to the effect of inertia as the bus has a greater mass of two vehicles.

options.text: Both will come to rest in the same distance
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect as bus having more mass and hence more inertia will come to rest after the car.
This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The distance they move will depend on their deceleration, which in turn would depend on the frictional force exerted by their respective brakes. If both were to experience the same frictional force 'F', then as per F=ma it is evident that the car having the lesser mass will decelerate faster ( greater magnitude of deceleration) and will therefore come to a halt sooner. Conceptually this is due to the effect of inertia as the bus has a greater mass of two vehicles.

questions.text: Which graph represents the motion of a car that is traveling along a straight road with a uniformly increasing speed?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Velocity of the object is increasing uniformly with time hence the acceleration is constant.
Acceleration-time graph – acceleration is increasing from a non-zero value.
Displacement-time graph – straight line with a positive gradient gradient is constant velocity is constant
Displacement-time graph – horizontal line gradient = 0 velocity = 0

questions.explanation: As velocity of an object is increasing uniformly so it's acceleration is constant.

questions.text: Two cars are moving in opposite directions with speed v. What is the magnitude of their relative velocity?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the relative velocity were 0, the cars would appear stationary to each other, which is incorrect for opposite directions.
Relative velocity cannot be v when both cars have speed v in opposite directions; it would mean one car is stationary, which is incorrect.
A relative velocity of v/2 suggests the cars are moving towards each other at a slower combined speed, which is incorrect.
The correct relative velocity is 2v because when two objects move in opposite directions, their speeds add up to determine the relative velocity.

questions.explanation: When two cars are moving in opposite directions at the same speed v, their relative velocity is the sum of their speeds: v + v = 2v. Hence, the magnitude of their relative velocity is 2v. The other options are incorrect because they do not account for the addition of speeds in opposite directions.

questions.text: The quantitative value of force is given by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton's second law provides a formula for calculating force, expressed as F = ma. It quantitatively relates the net force acting on an object to its mass and the acceleration produced.

Newton's third law is about the interaction between two bodies, stating that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. It does not provide a quantitative formula for calculating force.

Newton's first law discusses inertia, describing an object's tendency to remain at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a net force. It does not provide a direct formula for calculating force.
This option is incorrect because the quantitative measure of force is accurately described by Newton's second law of motion.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Newton's second law of motion. This law gives the quantitative measure of force through the equation F = ma, where F is the force applied, m is the mass of the object, and a is the acceleration. It establishes a direct relationship between these variables, showing that force is directly proportional to the product of mass and acceleration.Newton's third law, while significant, focuses on the principle of action and reaction between two interacting bodies and does not provide a formula for force. Newton's first law addresses the concept of inertia, outlining an object's resistance to changes in its state of motion, without offering a quantitative expression for force.

questions.text: Acceleration of a moving car when brakes are applied is _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the speed is increasing, the car has positive acceleration, but when the brakes are applied car slows down, the speed decreases.The decreasing speed is called negative acceleration. In both cases, the car is accelerating, but when speed is increasing acceleration is positive and when speed is decreasing acceleration is negative.

If the speed is increasing, the car has positive acceleration, but when the brakes are applied car slows down, the speed decreases.The decreasing speed is called negative acceleration. In both cases, the car is accelerating, but when speed is increasing acceleration is positive and when speed is decreasing acceleration is negative.

If the speed is increasing, the car has positive acceleration, but when the brakes are applied car slows down, the speed decreases.The decreasing speed is called negative acceleration. In both cases, the car is accelerating, but when speed is increasing acceleration is positive and when speed is decreasing acceleration is negative.

questions.explanation: If the speed is increasing, the car has positive acceleration, but when the brakes are applied car slows down, the speed decreases. The decreasing speed is called negative acceleration. In both cases, the car is accelerating, but when speed is increasing acceleration is positive and when speed is decreasing acceleration is negative.

questions.text: Two bodes with kinetic energies in the ratio of 4: 1 are moving with equal linear momentum. The ration of their masses is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect according to calculations in main explanation text.
Option B is incorrect according to calculations in main explanation text.
Option C is incorrect according to calculations in main explanation text.
Option D is correct according to calculations in main explanation text.

questions.explanation: Given:Kinetic energy ratio: KE_1 / KE_2 = 4/1Linear momentum: p_1 = p_2Relationship between kinetic energy (KE), momentum (p), and mass (m):KE = p^2 / (2m)From this, we can express mass as:m = p^2 / (2KE)Ratio of masses:m_1 / m_2 = (p_1^2 / 2KE_1) / (p_2^2 / 2KE_2)m_1 / m_2 = (p_1^2 / p_2^2) * (KE_2 / KE_1)Since p_1 = p_2, the momentum ratio (p_1/p_2)^2 = 1.The kinetic energy ratio is given as KE_1/KE_2 = 4/1, so KE_2/KE_1 = 1/4.Substituting these values:m_1 / m_2 = (1) * (1/4)m_1 / m_2 = 1/4The ratio of their masses is 1:4.

questions.text: Work is said to be negative when F and d are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Parallel. When the force and displacement are parallel, the work done by the force is positive.

Work is said to be negative when F and d are anti-parallel.Work is the product of force and displacement. It is a scalar quantity. The work done by a force is positive when the force and displacement are in the same direction, and negative when the force and displacement are in opposite directions.
Perpendicular. When the force and displacement are perpendicular, the work done by the force is zero.
When the force and displacement are at 45°, the work done by the force is positive.

questions.explanation: Work is said to be negative when F and d are anti-parallel.Work is the product of force and displacement. It is a scalar quantity. The work done by a force is positive when the force and displacement are in the same direction, and negative when the force and displacement are in opposite directions.

questions.text: An example of non-conservation force is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric force is a conservative force. The force does not depend upon the path taken and it conserves energy.
Gravitational force is a conservative force. The force does not depend upon the path taken and it conserves energy.
Friction force is a non-conservative forces. These forces do not conserve energy. The work done by non-conservative forces depends upon the path taken.
Magnetic force is a conservative force. The force does not depend upon the path taken and it conserves energy.

questions.explanation: Only frictional force is non-conservative. The work done by this force depends upon the path taken and the energy is not conserved.All the others are conservative forces.

questions.text: The area under a curved force and displacement graph shows:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The curved nature of the graph shows that the force is changing and is not a straight, horizontal, constant line; therefore this option is incorrect.
The curved nature of the graph shows that the force is changing and is not a straight, horizontal, constant line; hence, it represents work under a variable force.
A curved graph does not necessarily mean that the maximum force is being exerted because the pre-requisite for maximum force is a 0° angle between force and displacement, so this option is incorrect.

A curved graph does not necessarily mean that the minimum force is being exerted because the pre-requisite for minimum force is a 90° angle between force and displacement, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The curved nature of the graph shows that the force is changing and is not a straight, horizontal, constant line; hence, it represents work under a variable/changing force.

questions.text: When the applied force and covered displacement are parallel to each other than work done of the body is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. In this case, the work done is given by the formula:Work = Force x Displacement x cos(θ)Since the angle θ is 0 degrees, the cosine of 0 is 1. Therefore, the work done is nonzero and not zero.

When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The work done is calculated by multiplying the magnitude of the force, the magnitude of displacement, and the cosine of the angle between them. Since the angle is 0, the cosine is positive. Therefore, the work done is positive and not negative.

When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The cosine of 0 degrees is 1. As a result, the work done is maximum. This occurs because the force is directly contributing to the displacement, resulting in the maximum amount of work being done.

When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The work done is given by the formula:Work = Force x Displacement x cos(θ)Since the angle θ is 0 degrees, the cosine of 0 is 1. Therefore, the work done is not minimum, but rather maximum.

questions.explanation: When the applied force and the displacement are parallel to each other, the work done on the body is maximum. Let's explore each option:A) Zero: When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. In this case, the work done is given by the formula:Work = Force x Displacement x cos(θ)Since the angle θ is 0 degrees, the cosine of 0 is 1. Therefore, the work done is nonzero and not zero.B) Negative: When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The work done is calculated by multiplying the magnitude of the force, the magnitude of displacement, and the cosine of the angle between them. Since the angle is 0, the cosine is positive. Therefore, the work done is positive and not negative.C) Maximum: This is the correct answer. When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The cosine of 0 degrees is 1. As a result, the work done is maximum. This occurs because the force is directly contributing to the displacement, resulting in the maximum amount of work being done.D) Minimum: When the applied force and displacement are parallel, the angle between them is 0 degrees. The work done is given by the formula:Work = Force x Displacement x cos(θ)Since the angle theta is 0 degrees, the cosine of 0 is 1. Therefore, the work done is not minimum, but rather maximum.In summary, when the applied force and covered displacement are parallel to each other, the work done on the body is maximum. Hence, the correct answer is C) Maximum.

options.text: It was greatly good by you to proposed day's picnic at murree.

options.text: It was awfully good by you to proposed a day's picnic in Murree.

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. This option contains several errors: "off" should be "of," "too" should be "to," and "days" should be "day's." Also, "in Murree" is less preferred compared to "at Murree."

questions.explanation: Option C is correct. This option is correct. This is the correct option. It uses the right phrases and punctuation, and both "a day's picnic" and "at Murree" are properly used.

questions.text: The soup _ good
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: This form suggests an action but does not agree with the singular subject 'soup' and is not in the correct tense.
This is the correct answer because 'tastes' agrees with the singular subject 'soup' and is in the present simple tense, which is appropriate for describing a characteristic.
This form suggests an ongoing action but is not suitable here as it implies that the soup is currently being tasted, rather than describing its inherent quality.
While this is grammatically correct, it is not a common way to express the flavor of the soup in this context and does not fit the sentence structure.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Tastes.' In the sentence 'The soup tastes good,' 'tastes' is the present simple form of the verb, which is used to describe a general truth or characteristic. The subject 'soup' is singular, so the verb must also be in a singular form. The other options are incorrect for the following reasons: 'Taste' does not agree with the singular subject, 'Is tasting' suggests a temporary action rather than a general quality, and 'Has taste' is awkward in this context and not commonly used to describe food.

questions.text: The government has increased the taxes on all businesses in Pakistan.Many small businesses will have to close their operation in Pakistan.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and Statement II is the effect.
options.explanation: Here by reading out the two statements, it’s clear that because the government has increased taxes on all businesses in Pakistan (1), as a result, many small businesses will have to close their operation. Majorly because small businesses do not have capital large enough to pay increased taxes. Hence, Statement 2 here is the effect of Statement 1 i.e. the cause.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and Statement I is the effect.
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes.
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Both the statement I and II are the effects of independent causes.
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Here by reading out the two statements, it’s clear that because the government has increased taxes on all businesses in Pakistan (1), as a result, many small businesses will have to close their operation. questions.text: Verbal reasoningStatements(I) There is a sharp decline in the production of oilseeds this year.(II) The Government has decided to increase the import quantum of edible oil.

options.text: Statement I is the cause and II is its effect
options.explanation: Statement I is the cause and statement II is it’s effect because firstly there was a massive decline in the production of oilseeds due to which the government decided to import oil Thus only option A is correct

options.text: Statement II is the cause and I is its effect
options.explanation: this option is not suitable.Statement II is the cause and I is its effect: This is incorrect because the government's decision is a response to the decline in oilseed production, not the other way around.

options.text: Both statements are of independent causes
options.explanation: this option is not suitable. Both statements are of independent causes: This is incorrect because the statements are related. The decline in oilseed production directly influences the government's import decision.

options.text: Both statements are the effects of the independent causes
